initial_draft:
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I need to sell my 1 unit of X. The production cost was 40 ZUP, so I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP to make a profit. Player BLUE has offered ZUP to buy resources. I will propose a trade selling 1 X for 50 ZUP, which is a reasonable profit margin of 10 ZUP. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am willing to sell my 1 X for 50 ZUP. This covers my production cost of 40 ZUP and gives me a small profit. Let me know if this works for you. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All required tags are present and in the correct order. The proposal format matches the rules exactly.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Selling for 50 ZUP on a 40 ZUP cost yields a 25% profit, which aligns with the goal of selling for >40 ZUP.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. Since the user (Player BLUE) has not yet made a specific counter-offer, proposing a standard price is the correct first move.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason explains the 50 ZUP price, the answer is PROPOSAL, and the trade details match. The message is consistent with the action.
5. Rule compliance: OK. This is the first proposal, so I am well within the limit of 3 proposals.
</critique>
